title_en: Prayer in Al-Muharrar: Hanbali Salah Doctrine
title_id: Shalat dalam Al-Muharrar: Doktrin Shalat Mazhab Hanbali
content_en:
The prayer chapter of Al-Muharrar states the Hanbali school's positions on salah with the precision and economy of language that characterizes the work throughout. Because Al-Muharrar served as a teaching text for advanced students, its statements on prayer presuppose familiarity with the basics and focus on the positions that require careful attention — those where the Hanbali school has a distinctive view or where multiple opinions exist within the school.

The Hanbali school's position on congregational prayer (jama'ah) is one of its most discussed features. The mu'tamad Hanbali position — confirmed by al-Mardawi and present in Al-Muharrar — holds that jama'ah is a fard kifayah for the five daily prayers. However, the Hanbali school also gives significant weight to the view that it is fard 'ayn, a position associated with Ibn Taymiyyah the grandson and some other Hanbali scholars. Al-Muharrar states the fard kifayah position while the broader Hanbali literature records the ongoing internal debate.

The conditions of prayer in Al-Muharrar follow the standard Hanbali enumeration: ritual purity, covered awrah, facing the qiblah, entering the prayer time, and intention. The Hanbali school's position on the awrah of women — that it includes the entire body except the face and hands in prayer, with a view that even these must be covered — is one of the school's characteristic rulings, reflecting its general tendency toward caution in matters of covering.

On the witr prayer, Majd al-Din presents the Hanbali position that witr is a sunnah mu'akkadah (strongly confirmed sunnah), not wajib as the Hanafi school holds and not obligatory as a handful of Hanbali scholars have argued. The witr is performed as one raka'ah or three, not two consecutive raka'ahs without a sitting between them, and the Qunut supplication is read in it during the second half of Ramadan according to the Hanbali practice.

The prostration of forgetfulness (sujud al-sahw) receives careful treatment. The Hanbali school holds that sujud al-sahw is obligatory (wajib) when the cause for it is a wajib act, such as the first tashahhud or the Qunut, that was omitted or altered. This wajib character means that its deliberate omission when required renders the prayer deficient, though not necessarily invalid. Majd al-Din states this with the precision his school's position requires.

The prayer of the fearful (salah al-khawf) — performed in circumstances of genuine military danger — is discussed with the various forms the Prophet performed it in different situations, establishing the Hanbali principle that valid modifications of prayer in extraordinary circumstances are defined by the prophetic precedents, not by general reasoning about necessity.
content_id:
Bab shalat dalam al-Muharrar menyatakan posisi-posisi mazhab Hanbali tentang shalat dengan presisi dan efisiensi bahasa yang menjadi ciri karya ini secara keseluruhan. Karena al-Muharrar berfungsi sebagai teks pengajaran bagi para pelajar lanjutan, pernyataan-pernyataannya tentang shalat mengandaikan keakraban dengan dasar-dasarnya dan fokus pada posisi-posisi yang memerlukan perhatian cermat, yakni posisi-posisi di mana mazhab Hanbali memiliki pandangan yang khas atau di mana terdapat berbagai pendapat dalam mazhab.

Posisi mazhab Hanbali tentang shalat berjamaah (jama'ah) adalah salah satu fiturnya yang paling banyak dibahas. Posisi muktamad Hanbali, yang dikonfirmasi oleh al-Mardawi dan terdapat dalam al-Muharrar, menyatakan bahwa jama'ah adalah fardhu kifayah untuk shalat lima waktu. Namun, mazhab Hanbali juga memberikan bobot signifikan pada pandangan bahwa itu adalah fardhu ain, sebuah posisi yang diasosiasikan dengan Ibn Taymiyyah sang cucu dan beberapa ulama Hanbali lainnya. Al-Muharrar menyatakan posisi fardhu kifayah sementara literatur Hanbali yang lebih luas mencatat perdebatan internal yang berkelanjutan.

Syarat-syarat shalat dalam al-Muharrar mengikuti urutan standar Hanbali: bersuci, menutup aurat, menghadap kiblat, masuk waktu shalat, dan niat. Posisi mazhab Hanbali tentang aurat wanita, yaitu bahwa ia mencakup seluruh tubuh kecuali wajah dan tangan dalam shalat, dengan satu pendapat bahwa bahkan keduanya pun harus ditutup, adalah salah satu hukum khas mazhab yang mencerminkan kecenderungan umumnya ke arah kehati-hatian dalam masalah-masalah penutupan aurat.

Tentang shalat witir, Majd al-Din menyajikan posisi Hanbali bahwa witir adalah sunnah muakkadah (sunnah yang sangat dikuatkan), bukan wajib sebagaimana dipegang mazhab Hanafi dan bukan wajib sebagaimana diargumentasikan oleh sejumlah kecil ulama Hanbali. Witir dilaksanakan sebagai satu rakaat atau tiga, bukan dua rakaat berurutan tanpa duduk di antara keduanya, dan doa qunut dibaca di dalamnya selama paruh kedua Ramadan menurut praktik Hanbali.

Sujud sahwi mendapat pembahasan yang cermat. Mazhab Hanbali berpendapat bahwa sujud sahwi adalah wajib ketika penyebabnya adalah suatu amalan wajib, seperti tasyahud awal atau qunut, yang ditinggalkan atau diubah. Sifat wajib ini berarti peniadaannya yang disengaja ketika diperlukan menjadikan shalat tidak sempurna, meskipun tidak harus batal. Majd al-Din menyatakan ini dengan presisi yang dituntut oleh posisi mazhabnya.

Shalat khauf, yang dilaksanakan dalam keadaan bahaya militer yang nyata, dibahas dengan berbagai bentuknya yang dipraktikkan Nabi dalam situasi yang berbeda-beda, menetapkan prinsip Hanbali bahwa modifikasi-modifikasi shalat yang valid dalam keadaan luar biasa didefinisikan oleh preseden-preseden kenabian, bukan oleh penalaran umum tentang kedaruratan.
content_ar: The الصلاة الباب of Al-Muharrar states the الحنبلي school's positions on الصلاة with the precision and economy of language that characterizes the work throughout. لأن Al-Muharrar served as a teaching text for advanced students, its statements on الصلاة presuppose familiarity with the basics and focus on the positions that require careful attention — those where the الحنبلي school has a distinctive view or where multiple opinions exist within the school. The الحنبلي school's position on congregational الصلاة (jama'ah) is one of its most discussed features. The mu'tamad الحنبلي position — confirmed by al-Mardawi and present in Al-Muharrar — holds that jama'ah is a الفرض kifayah for the five daily الصلوات. غير أن, the الحنبلي school أيضاً gives المهم weight to the view that it is الفرض 'ayn, a position associated with ابن تيمية the grandson and some other الحنبلي العلماء. Al-Muharrar states the الفرض kifayah position بينما the broader الحنبلي literature records the ongoing internal debate. The conditions of الصلاة in Al-Muharrar follow the standard الحنبلي enumeration: ritual purity, covered العورة, facing the القبلة, entering the الصلاة time, and intention. The الحنبلي school's position on the العورة of women — that it includes the entire الجسد except the face and hands in الصلاة, with a view that even these must be covered — is one of the school's characteristic rulings, reflecting its general tendency toward caution in matters of covering. On the witr الصلاة, Majd al-Din presents the الحنبلي position that witr is a السنة mu'akkadah (strongly confirmed السنة), not الواجب as the الحنفي school holds and not obligatory as a handful of الحنبلي العلماء have argued. The witr is performed as one الركعة or three, not two consecutive raka'ahs without a sitting between them, and the Qunut الدعاء is read in it خلال the ثانياً half of Ramadan وفقاً لـ the الحنبلي practice. The prostration of forgetfulness (السجود al-sahw) receives careful treatment. The الحنبلي school holds that السجود al-sahw is obligatory (الواجب) عندما the cause for it is a الواجب act, مثل the أولاً التشهد or the Qunut, that was omitted or altered. This الواجب الأخلاق means that its deliberate omission عندما required renders the الصلاة deficient, though not necessarily invalid. Majd al-Din states this with the precision his school's position requires. The الصلاة of the fearful (الصلاة al-khawf) — performed in circumstances of genuine military danger — is discussed with the various forms النبي ﷺ performed it in different situations, establishing the الحنبلي principle that valid modifications of الصلاة in extraordinary circumstances are defined by the prophetic precedents, not by general reasoning about necessity.
